title: The Director's Table
subtitle: Any play · the production pitch. SCHEMA ONLY — content stubbed (design deliverable, not build).
adjudicatorOverlay: STUB. Shakespeare sits across the table as the shareholder who has to pay for your staging. He defends the text against your concept, choice by choice.

theories.label: STUB — the interpretive spine of your production

phases.label: Pitch the Concept
phases.instruction: STUB — commit to a single staging concept in a sentence, and the line it lives or dies on.

phases.label: Choice by Choice
phases.instruction: STUB — the shareholder presses each choice against the text; a choice the text won't bear is sent back.

phases.label: Textual Warrants
phases.instruction: STUB — cite the lines your concept rests on; each is ruled for how far the text supports the reading.

phases.label: The Table's Assessment
phases.instruction: STUB — an assessment of the concept's coherence with the whole text, including the scenes it can't account for.

resistanceAgents.label: STUB — the Shareholder
resistanceAgents.constraints: STUB — defends the text; rejects choices the play will not license.

admissibilityRubric: STUB — a choice is inadmissible if it requires the text to say something it does not.
evidenceRubric: STUB — rule each warrant: supports / consistent-with / contradicts the concept.
closingTemplate: STUB — assess the concept against the whole play; name the scenes it cannot account for; unused-evidence retrieval over the play.
